Investment - Why do business in Ethiopia?
Member of preferential trade agreements and bilateral investment treaties - COMESA, AGOA, etc.
Dedicated leadership with a vision to become a manufacturing hub in Africa by 2025.

Productive labor force with more than 60% of its population between the ages of 15 - 65.
Ethiopia has a clear vision for industrialization that builds off of its rapid growth

One of the top destinations for foreign direct investment standing 5 th in Africa.
One of the fastest growing economies with 9.68% average growth over the past 10 years.
Strategic sectors have been identified for investment including pharmaceutical and textile manufacturing

Potential to serve >$20 billion pharmaceutical market in Africa
Government 10 years expenditure for the sector (15B USD )
Out of pocket expenditure (46% of total expenditure)

12% of GDP to health (75% on essential medicines & services)

One of the first African countries to develop a national strategy for pharmaceutical manufacturing

Ethiopian pharmaceutical market has been growing at 15% per year and is predicted to reach $905 million by 2020

Source : Interviews, market research
Prevalence of non-communicable diseases: diabetes, cancer, hypertension
Rising healthcare coverage
High burden of communicable diseases: malaria, HIV/AIDS, TB
Awareness, diagnosis and treatment are increasing
Primary health care service coverage reached 100% in 2015
Increasing healthcare coverage by government to the large rural population creates growing demand for pharmaceutical products.
Growing population
With average economic growth of >10% per year, increase in disposable income will influence spending on pharmaceutical products
Second-largest population in Africa (>100M, ~8.5% of Africa's population ); growth of 2.3% per year is expected
Key drivers of pharmaceutical demand in Ethiopia
Large & growing population and rising health care coverage make pharmaceutical manufacturing an attractive endeavor

-Market access to USA and EU through free trade agreements
-Components and Trim manufacturing : producing components & decorations such zippers, buttons, plastics, tec.
-Managed to attract leading global brands such as PVH, H&M, Wuxi and Sunshine,
-Reliable green energy supply with an estimated cost of 0.04 USD/kWh.
-Garment manufacturing : producing any article of clothing such as dresses, suits, shirts, trousers
-Emerging as a regional hub for sourcing /contract manufacturers
-Booming industry which has grown 7 fold since 2008
-More than 2.7 million hectare of suitable land availability for cotton cultivation
-Textile manufacturing: producing any goods prepared by weaving, knitting or felting.
Ethiopia's textile and garment industry presents unique opportunities to investors

Industrial park facilities
Land is available at reasonable lease prices
177 ha service land for manufacturing
Established on 279 ha of land

Accessible to skilled labour
+4 million people live in the city
Population and employment
Addis Ababa; 25 km from city center, 863 Km from Djibouti port and 10 minutes drive from Bole International Airport
Kilinto Industrial Park is the first IP specializing in pharmaceuticals
One Stop Services (OSS) including processing & issuance of permits, licenses, registration certificates, agreements, tax identification number, customs clearance, banking services.
Health stations, fire brigade, expats accommodation and 24 hours security services
Dedicated power with own sub-station and state of the art waste treatment facilities with a dedicated park administration and support system
Ready for plug-and-play , equipped with all the necessary infrastructure including waste water treatment plants with joint calibration
Located along strategic economic corridors, connected to ports by electric driven railway lines and asphalt roads with Close proximity to abundant labor force

Full export duty exemption
Loss carry forward
One-Stop Shop Service under the EIC - including pre-establishment licensing and registration and post-establishment after-care services
Relaxed Industrial park land regime - 60-80 years charge-free lease for IP Developers, discounted lease rate for IP Enterprises
Regional regulatory harmonization underway to catalyze regional trade
Facilitation of market linkages
Exemption from duties and other taxes on imports of machinery, equipment, construction materials, spare parts, raw materials and vehicles
Guarantee for repatriation of funds
Fast-track medicine registration
Multiple entry visas for share holders and general managers
Guarantee against expropriation
Potential for long term procurement guarantee.
Exemption from income tax up to 8 - 10 years for enterprises inside IPs,
Exemption from income tax up to 15 years for Industry Park Developer,
Customs facilitation through bonded export factory and similar other schemes
Public procurement: 25% price preference and 30% prepayment for firms manufacturing in Ethiopia,
Expedited visa procedure expedited procedure of securing entry, work permit and certificate of residency
Fiscal Incentives tailored to investment
Non-fiscal Incentives tailored to investment
